data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can rely on bridge-outbound.py to handle FILE_IMPACT events explicitly with alerting on failure
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend bridge-outbound.py process_outbound() to handle FILE_IMPACT events explicitly — no silent fall-through. When the issue property PUT fails, write a BRIDGE_ALERT event with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED.

**Why**: The bridge must not silently drop events. Failures must be observable via BRIDGE_ALERT so operators can diagnose sync issues.

**Scope**:
- IN: bridge-outbound.py process_outbound() FILE_IMPACT branch, BRIDGE_ALERT emission on PUT failure with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED, unit tests for success and failure paths
- OUT: AcliClient._direct_rest_put() (Story 3dad-e87f); migration (Story E); consumer refactors (Story F); integration tests (Story b9ac-2459)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, bridge-outbound.py process_outbound() has an explicit FILE_IMPACT branch that does not fall through to the default handler ← Satisfies: "bridge-outbound.py process_outbound() handles FILE_IMPACT events explicitly — they do not fall through silently"
- When this story is complete, on issue property PUT failure a BRIDGE_ALERT event is written with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED ← Satisfies: "When issue property PUT fails, a BRIDGE_ALERT event is written with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED"
- When this story is complete, unit tests assert both the success path (property PUT + comment) and the failure path (BRIDGE_ALERT emitted with correct reason)

## Considerations

- [Reliability] BRIDGE_ALERT reason must exactly match FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED (consumers grep on this token)
- [Testing] Unit tests must mock AcliClient._direct_rest_put() at the failure boundary — not rely on live Jira

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
